title: DSDS Chunk Definitions
description: Defines documents for chunks. A chunk is a pre-composed block of code that represents a ready-to-use pattern in the design system — a copy-paste starting point built from the system's components. A chunk document holds the identity of the block, the code itself (`code`) and general document blocks for the rules around it (guidelines, use-cases, accessibility, and the rest). Chunks sit at the same level as components. Where a component documents a single building block, a chunk documents a composition of building blocks captured as code.

chunk.description: A pre-composed block of code that captures a design system pattern. Chunks give consumers a working starting point assembled from system components — for example, a layout, a settings form, or a confirmation dialog. The top level holds its identity (`identifier`, `name`, optional `description`), the `code` block that consumers copy, and `documentBlocks`/`agentDocumentBlocks` carrying the general block kinds (guidelines, use-cases, accessibility, content, sections, checklist) — so a chunk can hold agent-only rules like any other entity. Chunks are intentionally simple: they document a code composition, not the full anatomy/API surface a component carries.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a chunk.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier for the chunk (e.g., 'search-bar', 'settings-form', 'confirmation-dialog'). MUST be lowercase kebab-case and unique within its entity group.

name.description: Display name shown in docs (e.g., 'Search bar', 'Settings form', 'Confirmation dialog').

description.description: What this chunk is, the pattern it captures, and which components it composes. CommonMark supported.

code.description: The pre-composed block of code consumers copy to use the chunk. Provide it in exactly one of two forms: inline (`code` + `language`) when the source travels with the document, or referenced (`src` + `language`) when it lives in an external source file. The referenced form keeps long sources readable and lets a chunk share one canonical file with a live app. Consumers resolve `src` at read time — no build step inlines it.

oneOf.title: Inline code
oneOf.description: The source travels inside the document as a string.

code.description: The source code of the chunk — the block consumers copy and adapt.

language.description: The programming language or syntax of the code (e.g., 'jsx', 'tsx', 'html', 'css', 'vue', 'svelte').

oneOf.title: Referenced code
oneOf.description: The source lives in an external file the consumer resolves at read time.

src.description: A relative path to the source file that holds the chunk's code (e.g., './chunks/confirmation-dialog.tsx'). MUST be relative — the pattern rejects absolute paths, protocol-relative references, and scheme-bearing URIs (http:, file:, …). Resolvers SHOULD gate any remote dereferencing behind an allow-list, following the same rules a `$ref` fileRef obeys. The file is read verbatim — no build step inlines it into the document.

language.description: The programming language or syntax of the referenced file (e.g., 'jsx', 'tsx', 'html', 'css', 'vue', 'svelte').

documentBlocks.description: Structured docs for this chunk, as an ordered array of general document blocks (guidelines, use-cases, accessibility, content, sections, checklist). Chunks accept the general kinds only — they document a code composition, not the anatomy/API surface a component carries.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured docs for AI agents consuming this chunk, using the same general block kinds as `documentBlocks`. Content here is agent-only: hard MUST/MUST-NOT rules, verification checklists, and adaptation constraints an agent needs when copying the chunk into an application. Human-facing guidance belongs in `documentBlocks`.

relationships.description: Typed dependency edges from this chunk to other entities — chiefly 'composes' the components it is built from (mark `required` true for essential ones). This is the graph agents read for impact and alternatives. Inverse edges are derived by tooling.

metadata.description: Optional metadata: the shared entityMetadata fields (see metadata/metadata.schema.json). Declare the components this chunk composes as `relationships` edges, not links.
